header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches (C9500 and C9500X). Pass 2 of 2: catalog, groups, and models are all populated. C9500/C9500X are fixed 1RU switches (no modular chassis/supervisor/line-card concept); only C9500-16X (excluded, see design_notes) ever had a network-module bay, so the live lineup is fixed-uplink only. Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches Data Sheet
Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches Ordering Guide
Cisco End-of-Sale/End-of-Life bulletin for the C9500-16X switch and network modules C9500-NM-8X / C9500-NM-2Q
sources.url: operator-provided; not sourced from a locally committed document

design_notes: Pass 1 built catalog + groups only, with 'models' empty; Pass 2 (this revision) added the 9 models[] entries. All per-model validator checks now run (valid_primary subset, poe_budget_matrix max, access-port sum == total_port_count, etc.).

C9500 is NOT PoE-capable at all (core/aggregation switch line): every Pass-2 model will set poe_capable=false, poe_type='none', and an empty poe_budget_matrix. Simpler than the access-layer families in this respect.

No modular chassis / supervisor / line-card concept exists anywhere in this family: both the C9500 (UADP 3.0/2.0) and C9500X (Silicon One Q200) lines are fixed 1RU switches. The only model that ever had a network-module bay was C9500-16X, which is excluded (see below) — so the entire live lineup is fixed-uplink (uplink_modular=false); no network_module_groups entries exist.

EXCLUDED, per explicit ruling — do not model: C9500-12Q / C9500-24Q / C9500-40X (removed per the datasheet's own document history, 'Removed references to C9500-12Q/24Q/40X as the switch models are end of sale', Dec 12, 2024) and C9500-16X plus its two network modules C9500-NM-8X / C9500-NM-2Q (removed per a separate Cisco End-of-Sale/End-of-Life bulletin, operator-provided — not a locally committed document). Also excluded as structurally undocumented in either source: C9500-24X, C9500-48X, C9500-16X-2Q, C9500-40X-2Q — these appear as orderable PIDs in the ordering guide's legacy Table 2 but have ZERO specification data (no port density table entry, no PSU/fan matrix row, no SDM template row) anywhere in the datasheet, so they cannot be modeled without inventing data. Catalog items exclusive to the excluded models are dropped too: PWR-C4-950WAC-R / PWR-C4-950WDC-R (950W PSU pair, datasheet Table 14 shows these as C9500-16X-only) and FAN-T4-R (fan tray, ordering guide Table 3 confirms this is exclusive to C9500-24Q/12Q/40X/16X — all excluded).

Live lineup modeled in Pass 2: C9500-32C, C9500-48Y4C, C9500-24Y4C (UADP 3.0, 'high performance' tier) and C9500X-28C8D, C9500X-60L4D (Silicon One Q200) — 9 models total. Each non-X model offers both -E (Essentials) and -A (Advantage) hardware SKUs; C9500X-60L4D is Advantage-only — no C9500X-60L4D-E hardware or license SKU exists in either source document.

EXCLUDED, per explicit ruling — C9500-32QC is NOT modeled, despite being fully documented and not EOL. Its 40G/100G ports are physically paired (16 pairs across the 32 ports); each pair is EITHER 2x40G OR 1x100G (combining to 100G disables the pair's partner port), so the real constraint is a shared/combinable bandwidth pool, not 32 independent ports each freely capable of 40g or 100g. The port_group schema has no concept of a combinable-pair resource pool: any static representation would let a query like 'port_count where speed=100g >= 16 AND port_count where speed=40g >= 16' return true, which is physically impossible (max active ports drops from 32 toward 16 as more pairs combine to 100G). Modeling this correctly needs a genuine new schema concept, out of scope here — dropped rather than encode an incorrect configuration. See also DB/switching/THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md.

C9500-32C and C9500-32QC (excluded, see above) are the only C9500 models with no Cisco-labeled 'uplink' port subset — homogeneous 32-port switches. Since uplink_modular=false for every C9500 model (no NM bay survives) and the validator normally requires a fixed-uplink model to carry at least one role=uplink port row, C9500-32C uses a new opt-in model field, no_uplink_ports=true (added to this series' own switch-kb.schema.json and to selector/tools/validate-kb.mjs), which skips that check for this model only and requires zero role=uplink rows to be present. No other family sets this flag.

Registry addition for Pass 2: switching-axes.json port_speed.order gained '200g' and '400g' (Catalyst 9500X QSFP-DD uplinks on C9500X-28C8D/60L4D need speeds above the previous 100g ceiling) — additive legal-value growth only, no registry_version bump, same convention as the 'series'/'stacking_technology' additions.

StackWise Virtual (SVL) is Advantage-tier only (datasheet Table 24, 'High availability and resiliency': StackWise Virtual is listed under Network Advantage, absent from Network Essentials). Added registry enum value 'stackwise-virtual' (switching-axes.json) for this. SVL runs over ordinary front-panel uplink ports plus a license — there is no dedicated stacking cable SKU anywhere in either source document, so no stack_cable catalog/group entries exist for this family at all (catalog.stack_cables / groups.stack_cable_groups are empty arrays, and no Pass-2 model will carry a configurables.stack_cables key).

Licensing regime: only the legacy 'dna-term' regime (C9500-DNA-* SKUs) is modeled, per explicit project ruling. The newer 'unified' Cisco Catalyst Software Subscription (C9500-DNX-* SKUs) is deliberately NOT modeled — do not add C9500-DNX-* catalog/license entries without new sign-off. The legacy dna-term regime itself forks into two SKU buckets (not one per exact model): a 'standard' bucket (C9500-DNA-E/A-<term>Y) covering C9500-32C/48Y4C (32QC excluded, see above), and a 'low port density' bucket (C9500-DNA-L-E/A-<term>Y) covering C9500-24Y4C. C9500X has its own separate SKU family (C9500X-DNA-E/A-<term>Y); only 28C8D offers the Essentials tier, both 28C8D and 60L4D share the Advantage tier SKUs.

Fan trays are a genuinely new configurable category (no prior series modeled fan trays as selectable) — added to this series' own schema copy only (catalog.fan_trays / groups.fan_tray_groups / configurables.fan_trays). Only C9500X has a real order-time airflow-direction choice (C9500X-FAN-1U-R front-to-back / port-side intake, default; C9500X-FAN-1U-F back-to-front / port-side exhaust; max 6, must not be mixed). Every other C9500 model's fan tray is fixed and non-selectable (recorded on the Pass-2 model as included_by_default_non_selectable.fan_tray, a plain string — not a catalog cross-reference): C9500-32C uses C9K-T2-FANTRAY (max 5); C9500-48Y4C/24Y4C use C9K-T1-FANTRAY (max 4). All four real fan-tray SKUs are still recorded in catalog.fan_trays for completeness even though only the C9500X pair is referenced through a group.

PSU groups represent the complete orderable family per sub-line, not what one model's matrix needs (project convention): c9500-psus covers all non-X live PSUs (650W AC, 930W DC, 1600W AC, 1600W DC); c9500x-psus covers the C9500X pair (1500W AC/DC). Per-model valid_primary/default_primary subsets are set in Pass 2 from the datasheet's Table 14 matrix (C9500-32C takes only the 1600W pair; C9500-48Y4C/24Y4C take the 650W AC / 930W DC options). Both groups share the same secondary_none_option, C9500-SPS-NONE (the ordering guide's exact CCW SKU for declining a redundant secondary).

PSU default policy, resolved in Pass 2 per explicit ruling: every model's default_primary is its AC option, and there is NO secondary PSU by default anywhere in this KB — matching every other family's default policy. This is a deliberate departure from Cisco's own CCW ordering flow, which pre-selects a redundant (primary+secondary) pair and requires actively selecting C9500-SPS-NONE to decline it; that CCW behavior is not modeled here.
Out of scope, same footing as pricing — not modeled: the multi-unit bundle SKU C9500-48Y4C-A-BUN, and the RFID opt-in/out SKUs C9500-RFID / C9500X-RFID / C9500-RFID-NONE.

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

power_supplies.notes: 650W AC power supply (datasheet Table 12 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C, C9500-24Y4C (datasheet Table 14).
930W DC power supply, -40 to -72VDC (datasheet Table 12 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C, C9500-24Y4C (datasheet Table 14).
1600W AC power supply, dual input range 90-140VAC and 180-264VAC (datasheet Table 12 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by C9500-32C only (datasheet Table 14).
1600W DC power supply, -40VDC (datasheet Table 12 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by C9500-32C only (datasheet Table 14).
Catalyst 9500X 1500W AC power supply, 90-264VAC 47-63Hz (datasheet Table 10 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by both C9500X-28C8D and C9500X-60L4D.
Catalyst 9500X 1500W DC power supply, -40 to -72VDC (datasheet Table 10 / ordering guide Table 3). Used by both C9500X-28C8D and C9500X-60L4D.

licenses.notes: Catalyst 9500 NW and Cisco DNA Essentials license, 3-year. Applies to C9500-32C, C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C (datasheet Ordering information / ordering guide Table 2).
Catalyst 9500 NW and Cisco DNA Advantage license, 3-year. Applies to C9500-32C, C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C.
Catalyst 9500 NW and Cisco DNA Essentials 'low port density' license, 3-year. Applies to C9500-24Y4C only (12Q/16X also listed in source but excluded).
Catalyst 9500 NW and Cisco DNA Advantage 'low port density' license, 3-year. Applies to C9500-24Y4C only.
Catalyst 9500X Cisco DNA Essentials license, 3-year, for 28C8D SKU only (60L4D has no Essentials hardware variant).
Catalyst 9500X Cisco DNA Advantage license, 3-year, for both 28C8D and 60L4D SKUs.

fan_trays.notes: Fixed, non-selectable on the models that use it: C9500-32QC, C9500-48Y4C, C9500-24Y4C (datasheet Table 9 / ordering guide Table 3).
Fixed, non-selectable. Used by C9500-32C only (datasheet Table 9 / ordering guide Table 3, 'Catalyst 9500 Type 5 front to back cooling Fan').
Port-side intake. Default airflow direction for C9500X (datasheet 'Power supplies and fan tray' section). All 6 fans must be the same direction — no mixing.
Port-side exhaust. Alternative selectable airflow direction for C9500X. All 6 fans must be the same direction — no mixing.

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by id and inherits the whole set.

power_supply_groups.notes: Complete live PSU family for non-X C9500 (datasheet Table 12 / Table 14). Per-model valid_primary (C9500-32C: 1600W pair only; C9500-32QC/48Y4C/24Y4C: 650W AC / 930W DC) is set in Pass 2. Excludes PWR-C4-950WAC-R/950WDC-R (C9500-16X-only, excluded model) and C9K-PWR-650WACL-R (dropped per project ruling).
Complete PSU family for C9500X (datasheet Table 10 / Table 11), shared identically by both C9500X-28C8D and C9500X-60L4D.

fan_tray_groups.notes: Airflow-direction pair for C9500X, shared identically by both 28C8D and 60L4D. Default is C9500X-FAN-1U-R (port-side intake), set per-model in Pass 2. No other C9500 model references a fan_tray_group — their fan tray is fixed and recorded under included_by_default_non_selectable instead.

license_groups.applies_to: C9500-32C-E, C9500-48Y4C-E. (C9500-32QC-E also uses this SKU set in reality, but C9500-32QC is not modeled — see header design_notes.)

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

license_groups.notes: perpetual_member is null: the Network Stack (Essentials/Advantage) license is a perpetual entitlement embedded in the hardware PID, not a separately orderable SKU (ordering guide: 'Network Stack Essential or Advantage comes embedded in the hardware').

license_groups.applies_to: C9500-32C-A, C9500-48Y4C-A. (C9500-32QC-A also uses this SKU set in reality, but C9500-32QC is not modeled — see header design_notes.)

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

license_groups.applies_to: C9500X-28C8D-E only — 60L4D has no Essentials hardware or license SKU in either source document.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9500 High Performance 32-port 100G switch (QSFP28), fixed ports, no PoE. Network Essentials tier, DNA-term licensing, no StackWise Virtual.

ports.notes: 32x QSFP28 (datasheet Figure 3 / Table 3). Homogeneous — Cisco does not designate any subset as 'uplink'.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How resolved values were derived (for re-validation).
axis_value_provenance.ports: Datasheet Table 3 (port density) and Figure 3 caption ('32x 100 Gigabit Ethernet ports').
axis_value_provenance.default_primary: AC per project ruling (Cisco CCW pre-selects AC as primary); datasheet Table 14 confirms C9500-32C's only valid pair is the 1600W AC/DC options.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + per-model PSU overlay.
